commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

Fix RTL/LTR microcopy and strip M suffix from stored major chord names

- AudioChordDetector: add direction:rtl to all Hebrew text containers,
  wrap embedded English words (Start, Add) in <bdi> tags, flip arrows
  to ← to match RTL reading order, store chord via formatChordName so
  'CM' is saved as 'C' (no uppercase M for plain major)
- App: add direction:rtl to shared-progression banner span
- SongLibraryModal: add direction:rtl to empty-state paragraph, wrap
  'Save Song' in <bdi>
- LyricsTab: wrap 'Chords' in <bdi> inside Hebrew sentence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

added ATM LLD project with concurrency-safe design

Design patterns: Strategy (accounts), Template Method (transactions),
Factory Method (TransactionFactory), Observer (notifications).

Concurrency handling:
- ReentrantLock in CheckingAccount/SavingAccount for thread-safe balance ops
- Semaphore in CashDispenser for FCFS physical resource access
- AtomicInteger for thread-safe transaction ID generation
- BlockingQueue in PrinterManager for producer-consumer receipt printing
- Synchronized DepositSlot with volatile flag
- AtomicReference in AccountStatusManager for lock-free status transitions
- Ordered locking in Transfer to prevent deadlocks
- ConcurrentHashMap in repositories and SessionManager
- CopyOnWriteArrayList in NotificationService
- ExecutorService with timeout in BankCommunicationService

fix: remove hardcoded CSS brace count assertions from tests (#125)

Remove all `expect(opens).toBe(2143)` assertions and cross-file
reference tests that verified other test files contained the same
hardcoded number. This anti-pattern caused all 14 test files to fail
whenever CSS was modified. The balanced-braces check
(`expect(opens).toBe(closes)`) is preserved to catch actual errors.

fix: demote blowout_risk_under filter back to observation

Raw prediction HR = 57.9% (N=216) — filter blocks profitable UNDER picks.
Threshold 0.40 captures ~70% of players, too broad. Only 2 BB picks existed
(both lost) but N too small for conclusions. Keep recording for CF tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: ATR-based dynamic stops, drawdown scaling, equity curve filter

- ATR-based stop-loss (2x ATR) and take-profit (3x ATR, 1.5:1 R/R)
  adapt to market volatility instead of fixed percentages
- Drawdown-proportional position scaling: reduces size linearly as
  drawdown deepens (anti-martingale), minimum 0.2x at max drawdown
- Loss streak cooldown: pauses trading after N consecutive losses
  to prevent tilt/revenge trading
- Equity curve trading: backtest only takes new trades when equity
  is above its 20-period moving average (meta-strategy filter)

When an entry is an array, the fields are rendered side-by-side in a
CSS grid row, enabling same-line field layouts (e.g. firstName + lastName).

commit.message:
Fix chunk timestamp: use single timestamp per upload session

Each chunk was getting a different Date.now() timestamp, causing
the receiver to treat them as separate files. Now all chunks
share the same timestamp so they group correctly for auto-merge.

2) countEmployeesRecursive: grup node'larının adopted children'ını da sayar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

kind-pasteur-S40: H-maximizer Betti topology at n=3-8 (THM-099)

Complete Betti analysis of H-maximizers:
- n=3-5: all β₁=1
- n=6: 480 split 240 β₁=1 + 240 β₃=1 (same c3/score)
- n=7: all 240 have β₄=6 (conference matrix)
- n=8: ~80 split between β₄=1 and contractible
Odd n: uniform Betti. Even n≥6: split by spectral type.

commit.message:
experiment: full autonomous cycle — two agents, zero source code

Agent A: writes code → discovers 9 properties → sends fingerprint
Agent B: receives fingerprint → synthesizes code → verifies all

Results: 3/3 functions, 8/8 assertions, 9/9 properties verified.
No source code was transmitted between agents.

commit.message:
Fix drilling validation to accept comma decimal separator

Updated regex pattern in validateDiskData to accept both dot and comma
as decimal separators in drilling field (e.g., "5*114.3" or "5*114,3").

This fixes validation errors that were rejecting valid drilling values
from ЗАПАСКА provider that use European number format with commas.

Impact:
- ЗАПАСКА: +6 additional rows successfully parsed (2573 → 2579)
- No more "invalid drilling format: 5*114,3" errors

Add Thaibreak Elixir application with libthai NIF bindings (#1)

* Add Thaibreak Elixir application with libthai NIF bindings

Implements Thai word segmentation using libthai via C++ NIF bindings
built with the fine framework (elixir-nx/fine) and elixir_make.

- mix.exs: project configuration with elixir_make and fine dependencies
- c_src/thaibreak.cpp: C++ NIF using libthai's wide-char API
  (th_brk_wc_find_breaks / th_brk_wc_insert_breaks) with manual
  UTF-8 ↔ UCS-4 conversion for full Unicode Thai support
- Makefile: compiles the NIF shared library against libthai
- lib/thaibreak.ex: public API with break_words/1 and insert_breaks/2
- lib/thaibreak/nif.ex: NIF wrapper module loaded at startup
- test/thaibreak_test.exs: ExUnit tests covering word breaking

Requires: libthai-dev, Erlang/OTP 28, Elixir 1.19

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Expand test suite with comprehensive Thai word breaking tests

Adds 10 more tests covering real Thai sentences with English comments,
edge cases, and cross-validation between break_words/1 and insert_breaks/2.

- Specific word assertions for known sentences (greeting, food, weather)
- Parametric preservation test across multiple sentences
- UTF-8 string validity check on output words
- Cross-check that insert_breaks + split == break_words
- Word count assertions for known inputs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Add CMake build system to fetch and build libthai from source

- ExternalProject_Add downloads libdatrie 0.2.13 and libthai 0.1.29
  from GitHub releases (SHA256 verified)
- Both libs built as static PIC archives so thaibreak.so has no
  runtime dependency on system libthai/libdatrie
- Dictionary (thbrk.tri) generated via trietool-0.2 during build
- Makefile updated to drive cmake configure + build

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Ignore stale temp files from cmake probe commands

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Add GitHub Actions CI workflow

Builds and tests the project on ubuntu-latest using:
- erlef/setup-beam for OTP 28 + Elixir 1.19
- CMake to fetch and build libdatrie + libthai from source
- Caches Mix deps and CMake vendor libs for faster reruns

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Restrict CI push trigger to main branch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Fix libthai configure: pass PKG_CONFIG_PATH for datrie-0.2

libthai's configure script uses pkg-config to find datrie-0.2.
Pass PKG_CONFIG_PATH pointing to the vendor prefix so it finds
the datrie-0.2.pc installed by our libdatrie build.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Initialize g_brk in NIF load/unload callbacks

Replace the lazy std::call_once init with fine::Registration load/unload
callbacks, which is the correct place to set up and tear down NIF resources
per the Erlang NIF documentation.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Upgrade libthai from 0.1.29 to 0.1.30

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Rename Thaibreak.break_words/1 to Thaibreak.segment/1

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

* Add .clang-format config and format c_src

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AKJ76PF1kFvoVk7hNGw6vf

commit.message:
docs: 코드 리뷰 및 수정 기록 문서 추가

- 전체 코드베이스 리뷰 결과 정리 (BUG/IMP/REF/MINOR)
- 수정 완료 13개 파일 변경 이유 기록
- 미수정 사항 판단 근거 및 향후 권장 테스트 목록

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix output flag blocking, installer root cause failures, expand catalog to 31 tools

CRITICAL fixes:
- security.py: stop blocking --csv/--json/-o/--output (broke every
  Zimmerman/Hayabusa/winpmem run_command call). Output flags moved to
  separate _OUTPUT_FLAGS set; sift-mcp validates paths, not flags.
- installer: git pull errors silently swallowed ($cloneOk=true regardless);
  fixed control flow with $LASTEXITCODE check and Pop-Location in finally.
- installer: -Update always failed ("venv not found") because install
  creates .venv in $wintoolsDir but update looked in $InstallDir/venv.
- installer: FK download 404 — forensic-knowledge is a subpackage in
  sift-mcp (packages/forensic-knowledge/), not a standalone repo. Fixed
  with pip git+subdirectory syntax for both install and update paths.
- installer: uninstall/update couldn't find scheduled task or firewall
  rule — name mismatch ("wintools-mcp" vs "AIIR wintools-mcp").
- installer: join API timeout cleared API key then generated a new one,
  creating key mismatch with gateway. Now preserves key on ambiguous
  failures, only clears on definitive rejection (403/429).

Installer improvements:
- Add -Uninstall and -Update parameters (consistent with sift-mcp patterns)
- Mask API keys in console output (Mask-ApiKey helper)
- .NET detection uses --list-runtimes (works without SDK), recommends .9
- Hostname→IP resolution for firewall rules with re-prompt on failure
- -ErrorAction Stop on both New-NetFirewallRule calls
- Startup script includes AIIR_CASE_DIR/AIIR_ACTIVE_CASE for SYSTEM task
- Guard $overviewPath display with Test-Path

Catalog expansion (22 → 31 tools):
- New analysis.yaml: capa, yara, densityscout
- New collection.yaml: KAPE
- New scripts.yaml: Get-InjectedThreadEx
- sysinternals.yaml: +strings, handle, procdump (2 → 5)
- timeline.yaml: +chainsaw, mactime exec_type:script, alternatives
- memory.yaml: fix dumpit placeholder URL

Code quality:
- audit.py: CASE.yaml validation, fix docstring (40→20 chars)
- config.py: examiner sanitization aligned with audit.py (replace→hyphen)
- environment.py: add tool paths, one-level subdir walk for net9/x64
- inventory.py: cache find_binary results, feed catalog install_paths
- output.py: chunked SHA-256 (64KB) instead of full-file read
- response.py: align DISCIPLINE_REMINDERS order with sift-mcp
- Remove --stdio no-op flag, delete dead zimmerman.py (139 lines)

All 213 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
